Protein Carb is a nutrition company that sells protein supplements and carbohydrate products. Our refund policy allows returns within 30 days.

Use the knowledge above if relevant to answer:
odoo module structure

Here's a clear explanation of the **Odoo module structure** using the `custom_sale` module as a real example:

---

## 📁 Odoo Module Structure

Every Odoo module is a folder with a specific layout. Here's the standard structure:

```
custom_sale/                     ← Module folder name (technical name)
│
├── __init__.py                  ← Tells Python this is a package; imports sub-folders
├── __manifest__.py              ← The "identity card" — name, version, dependencies, files
├── README.rst                   ← Documentation (optional)
├── pyproject.toml               ← Python packaging info (optional)
│
├── models/                      ← Business logic (Python files)
│   ├── __init__.py              ← Imports all model files
│   ├── res_partner.py           ← Custom fields/logic for Partners
│   └── sale_order.py            ← Custom fields/logic for Sales Orders
│
├── views/                       ← User interface (XML files)
│   └── templates.xml            ← Form views, list views, website templates
│
├── controllers/                 ← Website/page controllers (optional)
│   └── main.py
│
├── data/                        ← Default/demo data (CSV/XML)
│
├── security/                    ← Access rights & user groups (CSV/XML)
│
├── static/                      ← Static assets
│   └── description/
│       └── icon.png             ← Module icon (shown in Apps menu)
│
└── i18n/                        ← Translations (optional)
```

---

### 🔑 Key Files Explained

**1. `__manifest__.py`** — The module's identity card
```python
{
    "name": "Custom Sale",           # Display name in Apps
    "version": "0.1.0",              # Version number
    "author": "Harshit Patel",       # Who made it
    "category": "Tools",             # Category in Apps menu
    "depends": ["sale", "website_sale"],  # Modules this depends on
    "data": ["views/templates.xml"], # Files to load
    "application": True,             # Shows in Apps menu
    "installable": True,             # Can be installed
}
```

**2. `models/sale_order.py`** — Adding fields to existing models
```python
from odoo import models, fields

class SaleOrder(models.Model):
    _inherit = "sale.order"          # Extends the existing Sale Order model

    x_name = fields.Char(string="Extra Name")   # New custom field
```

**3. `views/templates.xml`** — Adding fields to screens
```xml
<record id="view_sale_order_form_x_name" model="ir.ui.view">
    <field name="model">sale.order</field>
    <field name="inherit_id" ref="sale.view_order_form"/>
    <field name="arch" type="xml">
        <xpath expr="//field[@name='partner_id']" position="after">
            <field name="x_name"/>
        </xpath>
    </field>
</record>
```

---

### 🧩 The 3 Essential Files (Minimum for any module)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `__init__.py` | Makes it a Python package |
| `__manifest__.py` | Declares the module's name, dependencies, and files |
| At least one `.py` or `.xml` | Adds actual functionality |

Everything else (controllers, security, data, static files) is optional and depends on what your module needs to do.
